module.title: Week 20: Threat Intelligence & TTP Tracking
module.focus: MITRE ATT&CK, IOCs, STIX/TAXII, threat feeds

topics.title: Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework
topics.description: Map attacks to MITRE ATT&CK techniques
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Map attacks to MITRE ATT&CK techniques
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Map a ransomware attack chain to ATT&CK technique IDs
topics.interviewAnswer: As a matrix with tactics (columns - the 'why') and techniques (rows - the 'how'). Each technique has a unique ID (e.g., T1078 for Valid Accounts). Covers tactics from Initial Access through Exfiltration.

quiz.question: What does a tactic represent?
What does a technique represent?
options: The 'how' - specific method to achieve a tactic

quiz.question: What is T1078?
How does ATT&CK help SOCs?
options: Common language for attacker behavior and detection gaps

quiz.question: How many tactics did SolarWinds span?
options: Multiple from Initial Access to Exfiltration

quiz.question: Which best describes Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

quiz.question: What is the primary purpose of Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework?
quiz.explanation: Security principles like those in Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework typically span confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

quiz.question: Where does Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework apply in real-world scenarios?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework concepts are universal across all security domains.

quiz.question: Which certification exam covers Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework?
quiz.explanation: Most major certifications touch on Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework at some level.

quiz.question: Best way to gain hands-on experience with Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework?
quiz.explanation: Practical application in a home lab is the most effective way to build skills. Reading alone is not enough.

quiz.question: Which skill is NOT directly related to Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework?
quiz.explanation: While web development may use security concepts, Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework is more directly tied to risk, response, and monitoring.

quiz.question: Scenario: You encounter Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework during a security audit. What is your first step?
quiz.explanation: A professional approach to Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework during an audit is to identify the issue and document findings for further analysis.

quiz.question: What is a common tool used with Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework?
quiz.explanation: Depending on context, Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework can involve firewalls, SIEMs, or packet analyzers.

quiz.question: Understanding Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework helps prevent which type of attack?
quiz.explanation: The specific attack Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework prevents depends on its context within the security domain.

quiz.question: How does Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework relate to the CIA triad?
quiz.explanation: Most security concepts including Day 1: MITRE ATT&CK Framework can impact all three pillars of the CIA triad.

topics.title: Day 2: Indicators of Compromise (IOCs)
topics.description: Extract and format IOCs from incident data
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 2: Indicators of Compromise (IOCs) is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Extract
- Format IOCs from incident data
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Extract IOCs from a text description and format them in STIX-like JSON
topics.interviewQuestion: What are the types of IOCs and the Pyramid of Pain?
topics.interviewAnswer: IOC types: file hashes, IPs, domains, URLs, registry keys, mutexes, YARA rules. The Pyramid of Pain shows hash IOCs are easiest for attackers to change, while TTP-based IOCs are hardest to evade.

quiz.question: What is a file hash IOC?
options: MD5, SHA1, or SHA256 of a malicious file

quiz.question: What is the top of the Pyramid of Pain?
What is the bottom of the Pyramid of Pain?
What is the IOC lifecycle?
How many IOCs were shared after ProxyLogon?
Which best describes Day 2: Indicators of Compromise (IOCs)?
quiz.explanation: Day 2: Indicators of Compromise (IOCs) is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

topics.title: Day 3: STIX & TAXII Standards
topics.description: Write and parse STIX threat intelligence
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 3: STIX & TAXII Standards is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Write
- Parse STIX threat intelligence
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Create a STIX 2.1 JSON indicator object
topics.interviewAnswer: STIX (Structured Threat Information Expression) is a JSON format for sharing threat intelligence with Domain Objects (Indicator, Malware, Threat Actor) and Relationship Objects. TAXII is the transport protocol for exchanging STIX content via API endpoints.

quiz.question: What is an STIX Domain Object?
What does TAXII provide?
What is a TAXII Collection?
What US program uses STIX/TAXII?
What is MISP?
options: Alternative threat intel platform that exports STIX

quiz.question: Which best describes Day 3: STIX & TAXII Standards?
quiz.explanation: Day 3: STIX & TAXII Standards is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

topics.title: Day 4: Threat Feeds & Intelligence Sources
topics.description: Evaluate and integrate threat intelligence feeds
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 4: Threat Feeds & Intelligence Sources is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Evaluate
- Integrate threat intelligence feeds
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Compare threat feed sources and choose one for a SOC team
topics.interviewQuestion: What are threat feed categories and quality metrics?
topics.interviewAnswer: Commercial (Recorded Future), open-source (AlienVault OTX, MISP, ThreatFox), and government (CISA AIS). Quality metrics: freshness (age of IOCs), accuracy (FP rate), relevance (target industries), and volume.

quiz.question: What is an open-source threat feed?
What is a government threat feed?
What is feed freshness?
What happens without proper feed tuning?
What did the financial institution detect with ThreatFox?

topics.title: Day 5: Threat Intelligence Lifecycle & TTP Tracking
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 5: Threat Intelligence Lifecycle & TTP Tracking is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Apply the full intelligence lifecycle
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Walk through the intelligence lifecycle for a defense contractor scenario
topics.interviewQuestion: What are the 6 phases of the intelligence lifecycle?
topics.interviewAnswer: Requirements (what decisions need support?), Collection (gather data), Processing (normalize and enrich), Analysis (identify patterns), Dissemination (deliver to stakeholders), and Feedback (refine requirements based on input).

quiz.question: What is phase 1 of the intelligence lifecycle?
What is phase 2?
What is phase 4?
What is phase 5?
Who did the SOC track for 18 months?
Which best describes Day 5: Threat Intelligence Lifecycle & TTP Tracking?
